cards.text: RM14.79 billion withdrawn from EPF Flexible Account in 15 months — 4.63 million members accessing retirement savings for current expenses
cards.sub: Account 3 launched May 2024 as a permanent flexible-withdrawal facility. By August 2025 it had been used by 35% of all EPF members under age 55.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Post-pandemic EPF withdrawal facilities have permanently reduced the compound growth base for millions of retirement accounts.
cards.s: The 2020-2022 special withdrawals (i-Lestari, i-Sinar, i-Citra) plus Flexible Account 3 collectively pulled tens of billions from members' long-term savings.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Account 3 was framed as an emergency facility — but its design allows withdrawal at any time, for any reason, with no eligibility test.
cards.s: Members on the lowest incomes use the facility most heavily; Bank Negara has separately warned that household-debt-to-disposable-income remains elevated.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: EPF members aged 55 with savings below the RM240,000 basic-savings target remain the majority — Account 3 withdrawals add to that gap.
cards.s: Around 36% of active formal members met the RM240,000 basic savings benchmark in 2024 per EPF; the new RIA framework targets RM390,000 as the new basic floor.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Calling it 'flexible savings' does not change the math — every ringgit withdrawn today compounds into a larger gap at retirement.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Like eating your seed corn in a drought — it solves today's hunger but guarantees next year's famine.
cards.sub: Every ringgit withdrawn from EPF today compounds into a larger gap at retirement age.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Account 3 may help short-term liquidity, but its scale signals a household-finance crisis that policy should address upstream of EPF — not by raiding it.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

issue_headline: EPF Flexible Account 3 Withdrawals Reach RM14.79B in 15 Months
